message:
feat(planner): add Decision Contract with BLAKE3-chained audit log (bd-1lsfu.6)

Implement structured logging of query planner decisions following the
alien-artifact methodology: state/action/loss/calibration per plan.
Includes tamper-evident BLAKE3 chain, miscalibration alerts (>5x or <0.2x),
aggregate calibration statistics, and tracing instrumentation for
best_access_path() and order_joins(). 174 tests passing (16 unit + 10 integration).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -229,7 +229,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1lsfu.3","title":"Integrate EvidenceLedger for SSI decisions (galaxy-brain cards)","description":"Integrate the EvidenceLedger to record all SSI validation decisions as galaxy-brain cards. Every commit/abort decision must be auditable with full context: what conflict was detected, what the transaction's read-set and write-set contained, and why the specific resolution (commit vs abort) was chosen.

Galaxy-brain card format for SSI decisions:
1. Decision type: COMMIT_ALLOWED, ABORT_WRITE_SKEW, ABORT_PHANTOM, ABORT_CYCLE
2. Transaction ID and snapshot timestamp
3. Conflicting transaction(s) and their IDs
4. Conflict evidence: specific page IDs in the rw-antidependency
5. Read-set summary (bloom filter hash or top-K page IDs)
6. Write-set (all page IDs)
7. Decision rationale (FCW rule applied, safe snapshot shortcut, etc.)
8. Timestamp and epoch
9. BLAKE3 chain hash (tamper-evident append-only log)

The evidence ledger enables:
- Post-incident debugging: 'why was my transaction aborted?'
- Performance tuning: identify hot conflict pages
- Correctness auditing: verify no anomaly was allowed
- Capacity planning: abort rate trends over time

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every SSI commit/abort produces an evidence ledger entry
- [ ] Galaxy-brain card contains all 9 fields above
- [ ] Append-only BLAKE3-chained log
- [ ] Query interface: list decisions by txn_id, conflict type, time range
- [ ] Integration test: trigger write-skew, verify evidence ledger entry
- [ ] Evidence ledger does not impact transaction latency (async append)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:07.340613833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:26:17.696944116Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T18:26:17.696922305Z","close_reason":"Implemented SSI EvidenceLedger integration with async append, BLAKE3 chain, query filters, and integration tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["methodology","observability","ssi"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lsfu.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsfu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lsfu.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2kkn4.1","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1lsfu.4","title":"Set up proptest for SQL parser and planner","description":"Integrate proptest for property-based testing of the SQL parser and query planner. These are the two most complex components with the most subtle correctness requirements.

SQL Parser properties:
1. Roundtrip: parse(sql) -> AST -> unparse(AST) -> sql' -> parse(sql') == parse(sql)
2. Rejection: invalid SQL is always rejected (never silently accepted)
3. Determinism: same input always produces same AST
4. Unicode: identifiers with Unicode characters parse correctly
5. Fuzz: random byte strings do not crash the parser (no panics, no UB)

Query Planner properties:
1. Soundness: planned query produces same results as naive nested-loop evaluation
2. Cost monotonicity: better estimated cost plans never produce worse actual performance (statistical)
3. Join commutativity: A JOIN B produces same results as B JOIN A
4. Filter pushdown correctness: pushing filters below joins does not change results
5. Index selection: when an index is available and useful, the planner uses it

Generators:
- SQL statement generator: builds random valid SQL from grammar
- Random AST generator: builds random ASTs directly
- Fuzz input generator: random bytes and near-valid SQL

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] proptest integrated with SQL parser (properties 1-5)
- [ ] proptest integrated with query planner (properties 1-5)
- [ ] SQL generator covers SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE TABLE
- [ ] 10000+ test cases per property in CI
- [ ] Regression seeds stored in version control
- [ ] No panics on fuzz inputs (parser returns error, never panics)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:19.729523903Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:52:55.681489171Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T17:52:55.681469575Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["methodology","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lsfu.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsfu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1807,"issue_id":"bd-1lsfu.4","author":"PinkEagle","text":"Implemented comprehensive proptest suites for both parser and planner. Parser: 6 property tests (4200 cases) covering determinism, fuzz safety, unicode, incomplete SQL, multi-statement. Planner: 7 property tests (3200 cases) covering cost non-negativity, hierarchy, selectivity monotonicity, join ordering determinism, index cost, ORDER BY resolution, linear scaling. All 13 tests pass. Clippy/fmt/check clean.","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:52:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1lsfu.5","title":"CI artifact gates (baseline, flamegraph, golden checksums)","description":"Set up CI artifact gates that prevent merging code that breaks performance baselines, golden checksums, or other quality invariants. Each PR must pass all gates before merge.

Gates:
1. Performance baseline gate: run benchmark suite, compare against stored baselines. Fail if any operation regresses > 10% at p95. Artifact: benchmark results JSON.
2. Golden checksum gate: run test suite, compare output checksums. Fail on any mismatch. Artifact: checksum comparison report.
3. Flamegraph gate: generate flamegraphs for top-3 operations (by total time). No pass/fail — just artifact upload for human review. Artifact: SVG flamegraphs.
4. Coverage gate: cargo-tarpaulin or llvm-cov. Fail if line coverage drops below threshold (configurable, start at current level). Artifact: coverage HTML report.
5. Miri gate: run critical unsafe code under Miri. Fail on any UB detection. Artifact: Miri report.

CI pipeline structure:
1. cargo check + clippy (fast fail)
2. cargo test (unit + integration)
3. Golden checksum gate
4. Performance baseline gate (parallel with tests on dedicated runner)
5. Flamegraph generation (background, artifact upload only)
6. Coverage report (parallel with performance)
7. Miri check (nightly only, slow)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 5 gates implemented and running in CI
- [ ] Artifacts uploaded and accessible from PR page
- [ ] Performance gate catches intentional regressions (test with known-slow change)
- [ ] Golden checksum gate catches output changes
- [ ] Gate failures produce clear, actionable error messages
- [ ] Pipeline completes in < 15 minutes for typical PRs","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:33.005555048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:32:48.151520787Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","methodology"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lsfu.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsfu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lsfu.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsfu.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lsfu.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsfu.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1lsfu.6","title":"Implement Decision Contract for planner (state/action/loss/calibration)","description":"Implement a Decision Contract for the query planner following the alien-artifact methodology. The planner makes optimization decisions (join order, index selection, algorithm choice) that must be auditable and calibratable.

Decision Contract structure:
1. STATE: what the planner observes (table statistics, available indexes, query predicates, estimated cardinalities)
2. ACTION: what the planner chooses (join order, join algorithm, index usage, filter pushdown)
3. LOSS: what the planner tries to minimize (estimated query execution cost: I/O + CPU + memory)
4. CALIBRATION: how well the planner's estimates match reality (actual cost / estimated cost ratio)

Implementation:
1. For each query plan, log the Decision Contract as a structured record
2. After execution, log the actual cost (actual I/O, actual CPU time, actual memory)
3. Compute calibration ratio: actual_cost / estimated_cost
4. Aggregate calibration over time: should be close to 1.0 (well-calibrated planner)
5. Alert on systematic miscalibration (e.g., consistently underestimates JOIN cost by 10x)

This enables:
- Planner debugging: why did it choose hash-join over merge-join?
- Calibration improvement: identify systematic estimation errors
- Regression detection: did a code change make the planner worse at cost estimation?
- A/B testing: compare planner strategies on real workloads

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Decision Contract logged for every query plan
- [ ] State, Action, Loss fields captured
- [ ] Post-execution calibration ratio computed
- [ ] Aggregate calibration statistics available
- [ ] Miscalibration alert when ratio > 5.0 or < 0.2
- [ ] Integration with evidence ledger for auditing
- [ ] Dashboard or report for calibration trends","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:46.475966073Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:52:21.398442558Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["decision-theory","methodology","planner"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lsfu.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsfu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lsfu.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsfu.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- [ ] Dashboard or report for calibration trends","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:46.475966073Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T19:08:11.845604379Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T19:08:11.845583380Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["decision-theory","methodology","planner"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lsfu.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsfu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lsfu.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1lsfu.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2961,"issue_id":"bd-1lsfu.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented Decision Contract for query planner (bd-1lsfu.6):

Files created:
- crates/fsqlite-planner/src/decision_contract.rs: Core types (DecisionContract, PlannerState, PlannerAction, PlannerLoss, Calibration, DecisionLog), BLAKE3-chained append-only log, calibration computation, aggregate statistics, JSON serialization

Acceptance criteria met:

- [x] State, Action, Loss fields captured (PlannerState, PlannerAction, PlannerLoss)
- [x] Post-execution calibration ratio computed (compute_calibration, record_actual_cost)

Quality gates:

- cargo test -p fsqlite-planner --all-targets: 174 passed (164 unit + 10 integration)","created_at":"2026-02-13T19:08:06Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1lte","title":"Repl: Implement sender (systematic-first, repair stream, stop-early)","description":"Implement the sender side of spec §3.4.2.

## Behavior (Normative)
- Stream **systematic symbols first**:
  - send ESIs `0..K_source-1` in order (or in transport-optimized order)
- Then stream repair symbols:
  - send ESIs `K_source..K_source+R-1` and beyond if receiver still needs
- Support stop-early:
  - receiver indicates decode complete; sender stops streaming for that object

## Determinism
- Sender’s symbol generation schedule must be deterministic given `(object_id, seed, K_source, T, policy)`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests for:
  - systematic-first ordering
  - stop-early behavior
  - deterministic reproduction across runs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:54:59.364919251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T22:23:47.061339370Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T22:23:47.061317489Z","close_reason":"Completed sender behavior verification: systematic-first ordering, stop-early on ACK, and deterministic packet schedule reproduction with tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lte","depends_on_id":"bd-1jup","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1lte","depends_on_id":"bd-1squ","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1m07","title":"§5.9.0 Coordinator IPC Transport: Unix Socket Protocol + Wire Schemas","description":"Implement the cross-process coordinator IPC transport using Unix domain sockets, including framing, authentication, reserve/submit discipline, idempotency, and bulk payload transfer via SCM_RIGHTS (spec lines 9196-9515).

SCOPE: Enables multiple OS processes to route commit publication through a single coordinator. Required for multi-process MVCC. Covers socket endpoint setup, peer authentication, length-delimited framing, two-phase reserve/submit protocol, and 7 wire message kinds.

DATA STRUCTURES:
- Socket endpoints: foo.db.fsqlite/coordinator.sock (Native) or coordinator-wal.sock (Compatibility); directory 0700, socket 0600
- Frame: len_be:u32 (cap 4MiB), version_be:u16 (=1), kind_be:u16, request_id:u64_be, payload:[u8] (all header integers big-endian)
- Wire message kinds (V1): 1=RESERVE, 2=SUBMIT_NATIVE_PUBLISH, 3=SUBMIT_WAL_COMMIT, 4=ROWID_RESERVE, 5=RESPONSE, 6=PING, 7=PONG
- Permit: connection-scoped, single-use capability (max 16 outstanding default)

ALGORITHMS:
- Peer authentication: UnixStream::peer_cred() on accept; reject uid mismatch; optional MAC cookie from DatabaseId + per-install secret
- Two-phase reserve/submit discipline: RESERVE requests commit pipeline slot (permit_id or BUSY); SUBMIT_* bound to permit_id; connection drop without submit frees permit
- Idempotency: TxnToken (txn_id, txn_epoch) as idempotency key; same token returns same terminal response
- Bulk payload transfer: MUST NOT send full page bytes inline; WAL commits use spill file descriptor via SCM_RIGHTS ancillary data
- Canonical ordering: ObjectId arrays sorted lexicographically, pages sorted ascending, no duplicates

INVARIANTS:
- Frame len_be >= 12 and <= 4 MiB; reject outside range
- Unknown versions and kinds MUST be rejected
- Permit is single-use; reuse rejected
- Max 16 outstanding permits; excess returns BUSY
- Wire size caps: write_set_summary <= 1 MiB (must be multiple of 4), total witness+edge counts <= 65,536

TEST REQUIREMENTS (9 unit + 1 E2E):
- test_frame_round_trip, test_frame_validation (oversized/unknown version/kind), test_reserve_submit_discipline, test_permit_single_use, test_idempotency, test_peer_auth_rejects_wrong_uid, test_scm_rights_fd_passing, test_canonical_ordering, test_backpressure_busy (17th concurrent reserve)
- E2E: two processes, one coordinator; Process B sends RESERVE + SUBMIT_WAL_COMMIT; verify commit, crash retry idempotency, connection drop permit cleanup

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. Frame encoding/decoding round-trips all 7 message kinds correctly
2. Peer authentication rejects wrong UID connections
3. Permit lifecycle: single-use, connection-scoped, max 16 outstanding
4. Idempotent SUBMIT returns same response for same TxnToken
5. SCM_RIGHTS fd passing works for WAL commit spill files
6. Canonical ordering enforced on all wire payloads","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:58:20.309749866Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T23:51:35.329157106Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T23:51:35.329135556Z","close_reason":"Implemented peer auth + SCM_RIGHTS fd passing + tests; cargo fmt/check/clippy/UBS gated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1m07","depends_on_id":"bd-3t3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1m07","depends_on_id":"bd-3t3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1m07","depends_on_id":"bd-3t3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1m07","depends_on_id":"bd-zppf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1808,"issue_id":"bd-1m07","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §5.9.0 Coordinator IPC Transport: Unix Socket Protocol + Wire Schemas

### What This Implements
The cross-process communication protocol that allows multiple OS processes to route commit publication through a single coordinator. Required for multi-process MVCC.

### Spec Content (Lines 9196-9515)

**Socket endpoint (normative):**
- foo.db.fsqlite/coordinator.sock (Native mode) or foo.db.fsqlite/coordinator-wal.sock (Compatibility)
- Directory: 0700 permissions. Socket: 0600 permissions.

**Peer authentication (required):**
- UnixStream::peer_cred() on accept → reject uid mismatch
- Optional: connection-level MAC cookie from DatabaseId + per-install secret

**Framing (normative):**
```
Frame := { len_be: u32 (cap: 4MiB), version_be: u16 (must be 1), kind_be: u16, request_id: u64_be, payload: [u8; len_be - 12] }
```
All frame header integers big-endian. len_be >= 12, <= 4MiB. Unknown versions rejected.

**Reserve/submit discipline (two-phase, normative):**
1. RESERVE: request commit pipeline slot → permit_id or BUSY
2. SUBMIT_*: exactly one request bound to permit_id. Drop without submit → free permit.
- Bound: max 16 outstanding permits (default). Excess → BUSY.
- permit_id is single-use, connection-scoped capability.

**Idempotency (required):**
- SUBMIT_* carries TxnToken as idempotency key
- Same (txn_id, txn_epoch) → same terminal response

**Bulk payload transfer:**
- MUST NOT send full page bytes inline
- WAL commits: send spill file descriptor via SCM_RIGHTS ancillary data
- Uses asupersync::net::unix::{UnixStream, SocketAncillary, AncillaryMessage}

**Wire message kinds (V1):**
1=RESERVE, 2=SUBMIT_NATIVE_PUBLISH, 3=SUBMIT_WAL_COMMIT, 4=ROWID_RESERVE, 5=RESPONSE, 6=PING, 7=PONG

**Wire payload schemas (normative, V1):**
- Common atoms: ObjectId (16 bytes), TxnToken (txn_id:u64le, txn_epoch:u32le, pad:u32le=0)
- RESERVE: {purpose:u8, pad, txn:TxnToken}
- ReserveResp: tagged union {Ok{permit_id}, Busy{retry_after_ms}, Err{code}}
- SUBMIT_NATIVE_PUBLISH: {permit_id, txn, begin_seq, capsule_object_id, proof_object_id, pages_count, pages:[(pgno, commit_seq)], read_witnesses, write_witnesses, edge_refs, merge_refs}
- SUBMIT_WAL_COMMIT: {permit_id, txn, begin_seq, schema_epoch, spill_pages_count, spill_pages:[(pgno, salt1, salt2)], read_witnesses, write_witnesses, edge_refs, merge_refs}
- CommitResp: tagged union {Ok{commit_seq, marker}, Conflict{pages, reason}, Err{code}}

**Canonical ordering (normative):** ObjectId arrays sorted lexicographically, pages sorted ascending, spill_pages sorted by pgno — all with no duplicates.

### Unit Tests Required
1. test_frame_round_trip: Encode/decode all frame types
2. test_frame_validation: Reject oversized, unknown version, unknown kind
3. test_reserve_submit_discipline: Permit lifecycle correct
4. test_permit_single_use: Reusing consumed permit rejected
5. test_idempotency: Duplicate SUBMIT returns same response
6. test_peer_auth_rejects_wrong_uid: UID mismatch rejected
7. test_scm_rights_fd_passing: File descriptor passed correctly
8. test_canonical_ordering: ObjectId arrays sorted, no dupes
9. test_backpressure_busy: 17th concurrent reserve returns BUSY

### E2E Test
Two processes, one coordinator. Process B sends RESERVE + SUBMIT_WAL_COMMIT. Verify:
- Commit published successfully
- Coordinator crash during SUBMIT → client retries → idempotent response
- Connection drop before SUBMIT → permit freed
","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:01:07Z"},{"id":1809,"issue_id":"bd-1m07","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECTION: §5.9.0 (spec lines ~9168-9430)

PURPOSE: Implement the cross-process coordinator IPC transport using Unix domain sockets.

## Write Coordinator Overview (§5.9)
- Single background task serializing commit sequencing critical section
- Compatibility mode (WAL): serializes validation, WAL append, fsync/group-commit, version publishing
- Native mode (ECS): tiny-marker sequencer -- never moves page payload bytes
  - Writers persist CommitCapsule objects concurrently
  - Coordinator validates, allocates commit_seq, persists CommitProof, appends tiny CommitMarker
- Multi-process: coordinator is a ROLE (not thread in every process)
  - Exactly one process holds coordinator role (lease-backed)
  - Other processes route commit publication through coordinator

## §5.9.0 Coordinator IPC Transport (normative, Unix)

### Socket Endpoint
- Per-database Unix socket path:
  - Native mode: foo.db.fsqlite/coordinator.sock
  - WAL mode: foo.db.fsqlite/coordinator-wal.sock
- Socket directory: 0700 permissions
- Socket file: 0600 permissions

### Peer Authentication (REQUIRED)
- On accept: MUST call UnixStream::peer_cred()
- MUST reject any peer whose uid doesn't match database owner's UID
- Optional: connection-level MAC cookie from DatabaseId + per-install secret

### Framing (normative, length-delimited)
- Frame { len_be: u32, version_be: u16 (=1), kind_be: u16, request_id: u64_be, payload: [u8] }
- All header integers big-endian (network byte order)
- len_be >= 12 (header-only) and <= 4 MiB; reject outside range
- Payload encoding: canonical + deterministic; integers little-endian unless specified
- Canonical ordering: sets sorted with no duplicates (ObjectId arrays lexicographic, pages ascending)

### Reserve/Submit Discipline (normative, two-phase)
1. RESERVE: client requests commit pipeline slot → permit_id or BUSY
2. SUBMIT_*: client submits exactly one request bound to permit_id
- Dropping connection without submit MUST free permit
- Bound on outstanding permits: default 16 (same derivation as §4.5)
- permit_id is connection-scoped, single-use capability

### Idempotency (REQUIRED)
- Every SUBMIT carries TxnToken
- Coordinator treats (txn_id, txn_epoch) as idempotency key
- If terminal decision already produced → return same response to duplicate SUBMIT

### Bulk Payload Transfer (REQUIRED)
- MUST NOT send full page bytes inline in frames
- WAL commits: large write sets transferred via spill file descriptor (SCM_RIGHTS)
- Uses asupersync::net::unix::{UnixStream, SocketAncillary, AncillaryMessage}

### Wire Message Kinds (V1, kind_be values)
1: RESERVE, 2: SUBMIT_NATIVE_PUBLISH, 3: SUBMIT_WAL_COMMIT
4: ROWID_RESERVE, 5: RESPONSE, 6: PING, 7: PONG
Unknown kinds MUST be rejected

### Wire Payload Schemas (normative, V1)
- Common atoms: ObjectId (16 bytes), TxnToken (txn_id:u64, txn_epoch:u32, pad:u32)
- Tagged union encoding: outer tag is ONLY discriminant, no nested tag

#### RESERVE payload: purpose:u8, pad, txn:TxnToken
#### RESERVE response: tag(Ok/Busy/Err), body(permit_id | retry_after_ms | code)

#### SUBMIT_NATIVE_PUBLISH payload:
  permit_id, txn, begin_seq, capsule_object_id, capsule_digest_32
  write_set_summary (canonical u32_le array, sorted ascending, no dupes)
  read/write/edge/merge witness arrays (ObjectId, sorted lexicographic)
  abort_policy:u8

#### SUBMIT_WAL_COMMIT payload:
  permit_id, txn, mode:u8, snapshot_high, schema_epoch
  has_in_rw, has_out_rw, wal_fec_r
  spill_pages: [SpillPageV1 { pgno, offset, len, xxh3_64 }]
  MUST carry exactly one fd via SCM_RIGHTS

#### Response payloads: NativePublishRespV1 (Ok/Conflict/Aborted/Err), WalCommitRespV1 (Ok/Conflict/IoError/Err)

#### ROWID_RESERVE: txn, schema_epoch, table_id, count
  Response: Ok { start_rowid, count } | Err { code }

### Wire Size Caps
- write_set_summary_len <= 1 MiB, must be multiple of 4
- Total witness/edge array counts <= 65,536 per commit
- Any frame > 4 MiB MUST be rejected

### Internal Architecture
- Per-connection handler task translates wire frames to internal requests
- Awaits internal oneshot response, writes RESPONSE frame

PARENT EPIC: bd-3t3
DEPENDS ON: bd-3t3.5 (SharedMemoryLayout), bd-3t3.1 (Core Types)

Dependencies:
  -> bd-3t3 (parent-child) - §5: MVCC Formal Model (Revised)
  -> bd-3t3.5 (blocks) - §5.6.1 SharedMemoryLayout: Cross-Process Coordination
  -> bd-3t3.1 (blocks) - §5.1 MVCC Core Types

Dependents:
  <- bd-1onb (blocks) - §5.9.1-5.9.2 Write Coordinator Sequencers (Native + WAL Paths)

 {"id":"bd-1mbd","title":"5I.1: Diagnose index maintenance failure points","description":"# 5I.1: Diagnose Index Maintenance Failure Points

## Objective

Systematically identify WHERE in the code path index maintenance is failing. This is a diagnostic/investigation task that will inform all subsequent fix tasks.

## Investigation Methodology

### Step 1: Trace a Simple INSERT with Index

Create a minimal reproduction:
```sql
CREATE TABLE t (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT);
CREATE INDEX idx_name ON t(name);
INSERT INTO t VALUES (1, 'alice');
SELECT * FROM t WHERE name = 'alice';  -- Should use idx_name
```

Trace through each layer to find where index update is lost:

1. **Parser**: Does the AST include index information? (Probably N/A - parser doesn't see indexes)

2. **Schema/Connection**: When codegen asks for table schema, does it include index definitions?
   - Check `connection.rs` `get_table_schema()` or equivalent
   - Check `IndexSchema` struct population

3. **Codegen**: Does `codegen_insert()` generate IdxInsert opcodes?
   - Check `fsqlite-vdbe/src/codegen/insert.rs`
   - Look for index iteration and opcode generation
   - Expected opcodes: OpenWrite (index), IdxInsert

4. **VDBE Engine**: Are IdxInsert opcodes being executed?
   - Check `engine.rs` opcode dispatch
   - Add tracing if needed to see if opcodes are reached

5. **B-tree**: Is the index B-tree receiving insert calls?
   - Check `BtCursor::table_insert()` vs `BtCursor::index_insert()`
   - Verify cursor is opened on index root page, not table

### Step 2: Check DELETE Path

Same trace for:
```sql
DELETE FROM t WHERE id = 1;
```

Look for IdxDelete opcode generation and execution.

### Step 3: Check UPDATE Path

Same trace for:
```sql
UPDATE t SET name = 'bob' WHERE id = 1;
```

UPDATE should generate both IdxDelete (old value) and IdxInsert (new value).

## Expected Findings

Document which of these scenarios applies:

A) **Codegen not generating index opcodes**: Schema info available but codegen skips indexes
B) **Schema not providing index info**: Codegen can't generate what it doesn't know about
C) **Opcodes generated but not executed**: VDBE engine missing case handler
D) **Opcodes executed but wrong target**: Writing to table instead of index
E) **B-tree layer issue**: Index insert/delete not working

## Deliverables

1. Written diagnosis in bead comments identifying exact failure point(s)
2. Code references (file:line) for each problematic location
3. Prioritized list of fixes needed
4. Any quick wins that can be done immediately

## Files to Examine

- `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/codegen/insert.rs` - INSERT codegen
- `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/codegen/delete.rs` - DELETE codegen
- `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/codegen/update.rs` - UPDATE codegen
- `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/codegen/mod.rs` - Shared codegen utilities
- `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs` - Opcode execution
- `crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs` - Schema management
- `crates/fsqlite-btree/src/cursor.rs` - B-tree operations

## Time Estimate

This is a diagnosis task - should take 1-2 hours of focused investigation. Do NOT start fixing until diagnosis is complete and documented.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:46.171308952Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:17:41.654898668Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T15:17:41.654858663Z","close_reason":"Diagnosis complete: Codegen not generating IdxInsert/IdxDelete opcodes, Engine treats them as no-ops. B-tree layer ready. See comments for full analysis and fix plan.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mbd","depends_on_id":"bd-36eh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1812,"issue_id":"bd-1mbd","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Quick Start for Diagnosis

Run these commands to start the investigation:

```bash
# Search for existing index-related code
rg 'IdxInsert|IdxDelete' crates/fsqlite-vdbe/
rg 'index_insert|index_delete' crates/fsqlite-btree/
rg 'IndexSchema' crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/codegen/

# Find where INSERT codegen happens
rg 'codegen_insert' crates/fsqlite-vdbe/

# Check if REINDEX workarounds exist
rg -i 'reindex' --type rust
```

## Key Questions to Answer

1. Does `IndexSchema` exist and is it populated?
2. Does `codegen_insert()` receive index information?
3. Does `codegen_insert()` generate IdxInsert opcodes?
4. Does the VDBE engine execute IdxInsert?
5. Does the B-tree cursor have index_insert() method?

Document findings in a follow-up comment on this bead.","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:08Z"},{"id":1813,"issue_id":"bd-1mbd","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Diagnosis Complete: Index Maintenance Failure Points

### Summary: Scenario A Confirmed

**Codegen is NOT generating index opcodes** for INSERT/DELETE/UPDATE operations.

### Detailed Findings

#### 1. INSERT Codegen (codegen.rs:1911-2052)
- Opens only TABLE cursor via `OpenWrite` (line 1929)
- Emits only `Insert` opcode (line 2031)
- **NO code to iterate over `table.indexes`**
- **NO `OpenWrite` for index root pages**
- **NO `IdxInsert` opcode generation**

#### 2. DELETE Codegen (codegen.rs:2584-2675)
- Opens only TABLE cursor (line 2612)
- Emits only `Delete` opcode (line 2649)
- **NO `IdxDelete` opcode generation**

#### 3. UPDATE Codegen (codegen.rs:2370+)
- Same pattern - table only, no index handling
- Should emit IdxDelete (old value) + IdxInsert (new value) for indexed columns

### Additional Issue Found: Engine No-Ops

Even if codegen generated the opcodes, they wouldn't work:

```rust
// engine.rs:2085-2088
Opcode::IdxInsert => {
    // Index insert is a no-op in this in-memory backend.
    pc += 1;
}

// engine.rs:2099-2101
Opcode::IdxDelete => {
    pc += 1;
}
```

### B-tree Layer Status: READY

B-tree implementation is complete and tested:
- `index_insert()` exists at cursor.rs:1303 with tests
- `delete()` works for both table and index cursors

### Required Fixes (Prioritized)

1. **bd-3jjd (5I.2)**: Ensure IndexSchema available to codegen
   - TableSchema.indexes is populated at connection.rs:1914
   - Need to verify this flows through to codegen calls

2. **bd-so1h (5I.3)**: Wire INSERT to create index entries
   - Add loop over table.indexes in codegen_insert
   - Emit OpenWrite for each index root page
   - Emit IdxInsert after table Insert

3. **bd-qluy (5I.6)**: Implement IdxInsert/IdxDelete in VDBE engine
   - Replace no-ops with actual B-tree calls
   - Need cursor lookup and index_insert()/delete() calls

4. **bd-34se (5I.4)**: Wire DELETE to remove index entries

5. **bd-2f9t (5I.5)**: Wire UPDATE for index maintenance

### Files to Modify

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/codegen.rs | Add index opcode generation to INSERT/DELETE/UPDATE |
| crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs:2085-2101 | Implement IdxInsert/IdxDelete handlers |

### Verification

After fixes, this test should pass:
```sql
CREATE TABLE t (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT);
CREATE INDEX idx_name ON t(name);
INSERT INTO t VALUES (1, 'alice');
SELECT * FROM t WHERE name = 'alice';  -- Should use idx_name
```

Diagnosis complete. Unblocking dependent issues.
